content:
There are two major meteor showers coming up in the next 90 days:

1. Southern Delta Aquariids: This shower is expected to peak on July 30, 2026, which is 8 days from now. The ideal Zenithal Hourly Rate (ZHR) for this shower is 25, which means that under perfect viewing conditions, you can expect to see around 25 meteors per hour. To maximize your chances of seeing meteors, it's best to watch after midnight from a dark location with minimal light pollution.
2. Perseids: This shower is expected to peak on August 12, 2026, which is 21 days from now. The ideal ZHR for this shower is 100, which is one of the highest rates of the year. As with the Southern Delta Aquariids, it's best to watch after midnight from a dark location to maximize your chances of seeing meteors.

Keep in mind that the actual number of meteors you see will depend on the darkness of your viewing location and the phase of the Moon. It's always a good idea to find a dark spot with minimal light pollution and to avoid viewing during periods of high moonlight.

Based on the rise and set times for Elnath from your location (latitude: 8.7056, longitude: 77.0757), it appears that Elnath is currently below the horizon. It will rise again at 21:55:11 UTC on July 22, 2026, and will be visible in the sky until it sets at 10:40:39 UTC on July 22, 2026.

Please note that these times are approximate and do not take into account any local obstructions such as buildings or hills. Additionally, the times are given in UTC, so you may need to adjust them to your local time zone.

There are a couple of meteor showers upcoming, with the Southern Delta Aquariids peaking on July 30 and the Perseids peaking on August 12.
